reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21 — SPLADE correctness proven FIRST in headless Chrome (pasta example -> pasta/italian/restaurant + expansions noodles/italy/food; mars example -> mars/spacecraft/orbit + expansions martian/nasa/space), then the deployed page driven end-to-end (encode + expansion display + second-text sparse-dot match). Text-only demo. Distinct from the built dense-embedding demos.

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY + CORRECTNESS verified in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21.0 WASM) BEFORE building. The SPLADE pool (max over positions of log(1+relu(logits[v]))) yields the right sparse terms: 'A man is eating pasta at an Italian restaurant.' -> pasta 2.69, italian 1.98, restaurant 1.45 + EXPANSIONS noodles/italy/food (not in the input); 'The spacecraft entered orbit around Mars.' -> mars 2.57, spacecraft 1.83, orbit 1.58 + expansions martian/nasa/space/orbital. Deployed page (scripts/validate-splade.mjs 9/9): 31 weighted terms with expansions shown, and a related second text ('spaghetti and lasagna') scores a positive sparse-dot relevance (4.6) with shared terms. Real learned expansion, not canned.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread: a transformers.js AutoTokenizer + a raw onnxruntime-web session; the SPLADE aggregation is done in the worker.

rubric.evidence: text -> input_ids/attention_mask/token_type_ids -> MLM logits [1,seq,vocab] -> sparse weight per vocabulary term. A DISTINCT capability: LEARNED SPARSE retrieval with term expansion, vs the built dense feature-extraction demos (opaque fixed vectors) and vs the built rerankers (a single pair score). The representation is interpretable (you can read the weighted terms) and invertible (it maps back to words).
rubric.notes: The expansion terms (words weighted but never written) are exactly what separates SPLADE from lexical BM25 and from dense embeddings, so the demo highlights them.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (Splade_PP_en_v1, linked), task (learned sparse retrieval / feature-extraction), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 + a transformers.js tokenizer — noting transformers.js has no SPLADE task), the I/O, the exact SPLADE pool formula, quantisation (fp32), download (~532 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). No invented API.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only; the masked-LM logits + SPLADE aggregation are handled explicitly.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'devve1/Splade_PP_en_v2_onnx', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:532}). The ONNX is fetched THROUGH Cache Storage under a key carrying the model-id path, and the tokenizer files are cached by transformers.js under the same repo -> lib/model-cache.js auto-initialises on a returning visit with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download. Verified: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it flipped to ready and encoded the first example.
rubric.notes: 532 MB is a real download, so the never-silently-download guarantee + auto-init on return both matter; the per-model clear-cache control holds via the shared loader.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (absent) -> ready (auto-encodes the pasta example, shows terms + expansions) -> debounced live re-encode on either input + example chips; a second text triggers a match; a busy/queued guard coalesces rapid edits; error path via the shared loader + a per-run try/catch surfaced in the status line. Verified download -> ready -> encode -> match end-to-end.
rubric.notes: Empty text clears the cloud; empty second text clears the match. 550 ms debounce keeps live typing smooth.

rubric.evidence: A primary text input + four cross-domain example chips (food, space, tech-support, finance) + a 'compare against' second input that computes the sparse-dot relevance and shows the shared contributing terms. Two natural modes: inspect one text's expansion, and match two texts.
rubric.notes: The match mode makes the retrieval use case concrete — you see WHY two texts are relevant (their overlapping weighted terms).

rubric.evidence: Every term weight is the model's real SPLADE output; expansion terms are flagged by checking membership in the tokenized input (honest: a term is an 'expansion' iff it wasn't in the text). The match score is the true sparse dot-product, and shared terms are the actual overlap. Text-only demo, no media provenance concerns.
rubric.notes: Honest about the method: the code + UI state the SPLADE pool formula and that expansions are terms absent from the input.

rubric.evidence: The term cloud is role=status aria-live=polite; both inputs are labelled; a legend explains the two term styles; each term carries a title with its weight; expansion terms add a '+' marker AND italic style (not colour alone).
rubric.notes: Font-size encodes weight for scannability, but the per-term title gives the exact number for assistive tech; the '+' marker distinguishes expansions without relying on colour.

rubric.evidence: Full-width inputs (box-sizing: border-box), a flex-wrap term cloud + chips + shared-terms row. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate-splade.mjs), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Text-only layout; verified desktop+mobile. Subpage ladder is a recorded follow-up.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; a single encode is well under a second, but the SPLADE pool loops over seq x vocab (~30k) per text, and the model is 532 MB fp32 (no smaller export exists). Live editing is debounced 550 ms and a match encodes two texts.
rubric.notes: The ~532 MB fp32 download is the main cost (no quantized SPLADE export is available) - it's within catalogue norms (depth-pro is 572 MB) but a quantized export + a length cap for very long inputs are recorded follow-ups.

guidanceConsulted.query: run a raw ONNX masked-LM off the main thread; aggregate large per-token logits without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep interactions responsive (INP); debounce live input.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — encoding + the SPLADE seq x vocab pool run in a module Worker; inputs are debounced 550 ms with a busy/queued guard.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: sub-second per encode; page responsive while typing

guidanceConsulted.query: full-width inputs + a wrapping weighted-term cloud that never overflows horizontally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use box-sizing: border-box for full-width inputs; flex-wrap the cloud + chips.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inputs are width:100% box-sizing:border-box; .sp-cloud + chips + shared row flex-wrap.

guidanceConsulted.query: announce a changing result to assistive tech; distinguish two term categories without colour alone
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce the live result via role=status aria-live; label inputs; encode categories with text/markers, not colour alone.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the cloud is role=status aria-live=polite; inputs labelled; expansion terms use a '+' marker + italics + a per-term weight title.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: the encoded terms + non-zero count are announced; expansions marked with '+'

openQuestions: Would a multi-document ranking mode (one query vs several passages) demonstrate retrieval better than a single pairwise match?
Is a quantized SPLADE export available anywhere to cut the 532 MB download, or is fp32 the only faithful option right now?
followUpGoals: Add a batch mode: one query vs several documents, ranked by sparse dot-product, showing the matched terms per document.
Ship a quantized SPLADE export (if a faithful one can be sourced) to reduce the download.
Build a basics/practical/wild subpage ladder (single-text expansion; query-document matching; adversarial cases where lexical expansion helps or hurts vs dense retrieval).
summary: Learned sparse retrieval (SPLADE++) — a distinct retrieval paradigm (a sparse, interpretable weight-per-word representation WITH learned term expansion), separate from the built dense embeddings and rerankers, proven correct in the browser on ORT-web WASM (pasta -> +noodles/italy/food; mars -> +martian/nasa/space) and matched via sparse dot-product. transformers.js has no SPLADE task, so the masked-LM ONNX runs raw with a transformers.js tokenizer. ~532 MB fp32 (no quantized export exists; within catalogue norms). Index demo complete + verified 9/9; a batch ranking mode + a quantized export + subpage ladder are recorded follow-ups.
